paragraphs.text:
## Top 20 Ligands Ranked by log K (PbL formation, ~25 °C, aqueous)

| Rank | Ligand | log K (PbL) | T (°C) | I (mol/L) | Clinically Used? |
|------|--------|-------------|---------|-----------|------------------|
| 1 | 1,2-Phenylenedinitrilotetraacetic acid (PhDTA) | 25.46 | 25 | 1.0 | No |
| 2 | Pentaaza-phenanthroline macrocycle | 22.10 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 3 | **DTPA** (Diethylenetrinitrilopentaacetic acid) | **18.96** | 25 | 0.1 | **Yes** — FDA-approved as Ca-DTPA for actinide contamination; used off-label for lead |
| 4 | **EDTA** (Ethylenedinitrilotetraacetic acid) | **18.04** | 25 | 0.1 | **Yes** — CaNa₂EDTA is a first-line IV chelator for severe lead poisoning |
| 5 | trans-CDTA (Cyclohexylenedinitrilotetraacetic acid) | 18.00 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 6 | Triaza-phenanthroline macrocycle | 17.60 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 7 | EEDTA (Oxybis(ethylenenitrilo)tetraacetic acid) | 17.00 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 8 | N,N'-Bis(2-mercaptoethyl)EDDA | 16.85 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 9 | EOBEDTA | 16.80 | 20 | 0.1 | No |
| 10 | Trimethylenedinitrilotetraacetic acid | 16.50 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 11 | Tetraaza-phenanthroline macrocycle | 16.30 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 12 | DOTA (Cyclen-tetraacetic acid) | 15.70 | 25 | 0.1 | MRI contrast carrier only |
| 13 | **HEDTA** | 15.60 | 25 | 0.1 | No (industrial) |
| 14 | TEDTA | 15.50 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 15 | HBED | 15.10 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 16 | TRITA | 14.60 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 17 | NTA (Nitrilotriacetic acid) | 11.39 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 18 | Iminodiacetic acid (IDA) | 7.60 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 19 | L-Cysteine methyl ester | 6.60 | 25 | 0.1 | No |
| 20 | 3-Mercaptopropane-1,2-diol (thioglycerol) | 6.40 | 25 | 0.5 | No |

*Source: NIST SRD-46 (Critical Stability Constants database). All values are for the 1:1 PbL species in aqueous solution.*

paragraphs.text:
## Clinically Used Chelators Found in the Database

| Chelator | Common / Trade Name | log K (PbL) | Route | Primary Clinical Indication |
|----------|---------------------|-------------|-------|-----------------------------|
| **CaNa₂EDTA** | Versenate® | **18.04** | IV | Severe lead poisoning (BLL > 70 µg/dL) |
| **DTPA** | Pentetate calcium | **18.96** | IV | Actinide/transuranium contamination; off-label for Pb |
| **D-Penicillamine** (ligand_5857) | Cuprimine® | *Present in DB (91 VLM records)* | Oral | Mild-moderate lead poisoning (second-line); Wilson's disease |
| **DMSA / Succimer** (ligand_8997, meso-dithiotartaric acid) | Chemet® | *Present in DB (41 VLM records)* | Oral | First-line oral chelator for childhood lead poisoning |
| **BAL / Dimercaprol** (ligand_9759) | British Anti-Lewisite | *Present in DB (25 VLM records)* | IM | Severe lead encephalopathy (used with CaNa₂EDTA) |

> **Note:** D-Penicillamine, DMSA (succimer), and BAL (dimercaprol) are present in the SRD-46 database with Pb²⁺ data, but their specific log K values for Pb²⁺ did not appear in the top-ranked results above. Their Pb²⁺ stability constants are generally lower than those of the aminopolycarboxylate chelators (EDTA, DTPA), which is consistent with the known chemistry — thiol-based chelators derive their clinical efficacy partly from favorable biodistribution and oral bioavailability rather than from the highest thermodynamic stability alone. DMPS (unithiol), another clinical chelator, was **not found** in the SRD-46 database.

paragraphs.text:
## Chemistry Insights

1. **Aminopolycarboxylates dominate thermodynamic affinity.** EDTA and DTPA form the most stable 1:1 complexes with Pb²⁺ (log K ≈ 18–19) among clinically relevant agents. Their hexadentate/octadentate coordination saturates Pb²⁺'s coordination sphere, yielding very high formation constants.

2. **Thiol-based chelators trade thermodynamic strength for pharmacological advantages.** DMSA (succimer) and BAL have lower log K values for Pb²⁺ but are preferred clinically because they can be given orally (DMSA) or intramuscularly (BAL), cross cell membranes more readily, and are less likely to redistribute lead to the brain — a critical safety consideration in pediatric patients.

3. **The highest-affinity ligand overall — PhDTA (log K = 25.46) — is not used clinically** due to poor water solubility and toxicity concerns. This illustrates that thermodynamic stability alone does not determine clinical utility; selectivity over essential metals (Ca²⁺, Zn²⁺, Fe³⁺), pharmacokinetics, and safety profile are equally important in chelation therapy design.

⚠️ *This is a scientific data summary from NIST SRD-46, not medical advice. Chelation therapy protocols should be designed by qualified toxicologists/physicians.*
